info.title: Instantly Service
info.description: Cold email outreach service via Instantly.ai API. Manages campaigns, leads, accounts, analytics, and webhook events.

subject.description: Shared subject for all steps in the sequence

daysSinceLastStep.description: Delay in days since the previous step (0 = immediate)

sequence.description: Ordered email steps — at least one required

emailsContacted.description: Leads added to a campaign (row exists in instantly_campaigns, immediate)

emailsSent.description: Total email_sent events (confirmed by Instantly webhook)

emailsOpened.description: Unique recipients who opened (COUNT DISTINCT lead_email with email_opened events)

recipients.description: Unique recipients (COUNT DISTINCT lead_email with email_sent events)

stepStats.description: Per-step breakdown (only present when step data exists)

emailsContacted.description: Leads added to a campaign (row exists in instantly_campaigns, immediate)

emailsSent.description: Total email_sent events (confirmed by Instantly webhook)

emailsOpened.description: Unique recipients who opened (COUNT DISTINCT lead_email with email_opened events)

leadId.description: Lead ID found in instantly_campaigns for this email

replyClassification.description: Reply classification based on Instantly interest status. null = no reply
Reply classification based on Instantly interest status. null = no reply

campaignId.description: Campaign ID to scope campaign-level results (omit for global-only checks)

get.summary: Get webhook URL for BYOK configuration
get.description: Returns the webhook URL that BYOK customers should paste into their Instantly dashboard webhook settings.

post.description: Verification: the campaign_id in the payload must exist in the database. Each campaign UUID is unguessable and stored with its org on creation.
Brand IDs, campaign ID, and workflow slug are read from headers (x-brand-id, x-campaign-id, x-workflow-slug) — do NOT pass them in the body.

schema.description: Campaign ID — automatically injected by workflow-service on all DAG calls

parameters.description: Campaign ID — automatically injected by workflow-service on all DAG calls
parameters.name: x-campaign-id

parameters.name: x-brand-id

parameters.description: Workflow slug — automatically injected by workflow-service on all DAG calls
parameters.name: x-workflow-slug

schema.description: Feature slug — propagated through the full call chain for tracking

parameters.description: Feature slug — propagated through the full call chain for tracking
parameters.name: x-feature-slug

post.description: Checks all active campaigns against the Instantly API to detect error states. For each errored campaign: updates DB status, cancels provisioned costs, fails the run, and sends an admin notification.

get.summary: Get aggregated stats (no identity headers required)

post.summary: Get stats grouped by sets of run IDs
post.description: Accepts named groups of run IDs and returns aggregated stats per group in a single call. Used by the leaderboard to fetch per-workflow stats.

post.summary: Batch delivery status check for leads/emails
post.description: Returns campaign-scoped, brand-scoped, and global delivery status for each email. Campaign scope is null if campaignId omitted; brand scope is null if x-brand-id header omitted; global aggregates across everything. Each result includes the leadId found in the database for that email.
